logoLabel.description: Label of the logo The default label for Commerzbank logo is "Commerzbank Logo" and the default label for custom logo is "Company Logo" (en) or "Unternehmenslogo" (de) If the logoLabel is set to "⎵" the image will be hidden for assistive technologies (rare purely decorative cases). In case of internal Coba-logos, the SVG-title will set the `aria-labelledby`-attribute of the SVG element. If the logo is a custom image, the `alt`-attribute is used (alt is specific to `` elements). If the logo shall be active, `aria-label` is also used on the `a` or `button element`. (aria-label is generic and can be applied to other elements than `img`.)
logoLabel.name: logoLabel

isMonochrome.description: Logo and text monochrome (only available for default Commerzbank logo)
isMonochrome.name: isMonochrome

variant.description: Logo variant (only available for default Commerzbank logo) - Use one of "vertical", "horizontal" or "mobile" (default).
variant.name: variant

fitToBox.description: Resize logo to fit its parent container
fitToBox.name: fitToBox

srcCustom.description: Image source for a custom logo.
srcCustom.name: srcCustom

disabled.description: If set to true, the logo is rendered as an element without a surrounding action element (e.g. a link or a button).
disabled.name: disabled

href.description: URL of the link (e.g. home page) that will be opened on logo click. An 'a'-tag will be rendered.
href.name: href

htmlAttrs.description:
Additional (action-)HTML attributes can be added here, e.g. target, tabindex or a different aria-label for the
link or button element (these attributes are routed to the surrounding action not to the image).
htmlAttrs.name: htmlAttrs

name.description: The name of the slider e.g. for forms
name.name: name

value.description:
The current value of the slider. The value is (should at least be) between minValue and maxValue
In a multi range look the value contains a lower and upper value within a tupel type [lower, upper], otherwise single value
value.name: value

label.description: The label (at the top left side)
label.name: label

ariaLabel.description: Aria Label - either a string (single slider) or an array with two string values (range slider)
ariaLabel.name: ariaLabel

ariaValueTextFormatter.description: Formatter for current value, screen reader friendly. In a multi range look, map toan array with the current lower and upper aria value text e.g. ["1000 €", "3000 €"]
ariaValueTextFormatter.name: ariaValueTextFormatter

disabled.description: Disables all actions
disabled.name: disabled

step.description: A fraction of maxValue - minValue. The steps are marked on the slider.
step.name: step

precision.description:
The precision - If the precision is 1, the slider gives back integers only; if precision = 0.01,
there are 2 decimals of each value. You can also add a precision of 100
to enforce values (e.g. Euros) to be divisible by 100, no precision provides more than 2 decimals
precision.name: precision

helperMinValue.description: This is a helper text appearing at the bottom left side to indicate the minimum Value. Could also contain JSX
helperMinValue.name: helperMinValue

helperMaxValue.description: This is a helper text appearing at the bottom rigth side to indicate the maximum Value. Could also contain JSX
helperMaxValue.name: helperMaxValue

minValue.description: The minimal value of the slider
minValue.name: minValue

maxValue.description: The maximal value of the slider
maxValue.name: maxValue

htmlAttrs.description: Object with additional html attributs for the slider. Also, you can provide a tabIndex like.
htmlAttrs.name: htmlAttrs

look.description: determine the type of slider basically
look.name: look

showThumb.description: @deprecated - do not use, for internal purposes only
showThumb.name: showThumb

ref.name: ref

onChange.description: Called on change (after key change or letting go of the slider). Use this function to set the new value of the slider. The value contains one value on single slider, two on range slider with [lower, upper]
onChange.name: onChange

onInput.description: Called on drag. Similar to onChange, but is also called while dragging the slider. The value contains one value on single slider, two on range slider with [lower, upper]
onInput.name: onInput

distanceFactor.description:
For internal use only:
The distance factor can manage the approximation between 2 thumbs. The calculation is a result of
factor multiplied with thumb width. The default is set on 0.0. The value > 0 hasn't an effect on range
slider with step or precision (precision > 1).
distanceFactor.name: distanceFactor

loadingText.description:
This prop is used to provide an ARIA label for the Spinner component to inform screen reader users about the current status of processes.
If not set, a fixed text ("Loading"/"Laden...") will be read by screen readers. Note that this text (also visible beneath the Spinner circle with reduced-motion browser settings)
cannot be modified to avoid potential UX issues caused by too long text.

description:
Comprehensive Checkbox automation test story
Tests all checkbox scenarios including single checkboxes, groups, states, and accessibility features

Interactive Checkbox with configurable properties.
Use the Controls panel to modify the component.
Note: Has a11y violation in Storybook. See CheckboxDefault.example.tsx for correct implementation.

value.description:
Setting the value to true/false will check/uncheck the Checkbox. Setting the value to undefined will
generate the indeterminate look of the Checkbox (which is not meant to be activated by a user clicking on or
pressing the Space key on a Checkbox). For information about indeterminate use cases,
please see https://css-tricks.com/indeterminate-checkboxes/ and
https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Element/input/checkbox.
value.name: value

disabled.description: Boolean value indicating if Checkbox input is disabled
disabled.name: disabled

invalid.description: Boolean value indicating if Checkbox input is invalid
invalid.name: invalid

label.description: Text on the right side of the Checkbox
label.name: label

name.description:
You can set the internal name attribute for a Checkbox with this prop. Several checkboxes with the same name
will be grouped together. For more usage information, see
https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Element/input/checkbox.
name.name: name

helperText.description: A small helper text that is placed below the label.
helperText.name: helperText

errorText.description:
Error text below the label. If you set this prop, the Checkbox is turned to invalid, even if the invalid
prop is false.
errorText.name: errorText

onChange.description: Function to be called whenever the checkbox is clicked. Not called when the checkbox is disabled.
onChange.name: onChange

type.name: (value: boolean, name: string, event: SyntheticEvent<HTMLInputElement, Event>) => void

htmlAttrs.name: htmlAttrs

errorTextAriaLive.description: Use this prop to control if the aria-live attribute is set for the errorText
errorTextAriaLive.name: errorTextAriaLive

id.name: id

className.name: className

children.name: children

direction.description: Layout of the inputs, vertical or horizontal
direction.name: direction

label.description:
You can set a small title text for the whole group above your first item with
this prop. Using this prop also internally sets the aria-labelledby attribute. As a result, your label prop
value will be linked to the fieldset HTML element of the group by aria-labelledby. (However, setting an
additional aria-labelledby in the htmlAttrs prop of your group will overwrite the aria-labelledby set by
your label prop.)
label.name: label

helperText.description: Sets a helper text below the component.
helperText.name: helperText

errorText.description: Adds an error text below the component. If you use this prop, the errorText is added below the helperText (if defined), and the group is implicitly set to invalid.
errorText.name: errorText

invalid.description: Indicates if this group is valid
invalid.name: invalid

direction.description:
Controls the layout and behavior of the Radio Chips group:
- "wrap": Chips wrap automatically to another line when space runs out.
- "scroll": Chips are displayed in a single horizontal line with a scroll container for overflow, ideal for mobile viewports.
- "condensed": Chips are grouped together within a border with no gap between items, displayed in a horizontal scrollable container.
direction.name: direction

clearIcon.description:
If clearIcon is true, an X-icon is displayed for non-empty/selected fields on focus and hover, in order to reset
a selection. A click will call the 'onChange' method -> with an empty string - default is "true".
clearIcon.name: clearIcon

name.description: Sets the 'name'-attribute of the input.
name.name: name

minDate.description:
Date Object with the earliest date that can be selected. The minimum date is included in the selectable range.
Ensure, that minDate <= maxDate. Only year, month and day information are used, the time information is ignored.
minDate.name: minDate

maxDate.description:
Date Object with the latest date that can be selected. The maximum date is included in the selectable range.
Ensure, that minDate <= maxDate. Only year, month and day information are used, the time information is ignored.
maxDate.name: maxDate

disabledDates.description:
Set the disabledDates list which contains e.g. the business holiday days.
Days contained in the list will be displayed disabled in the dropdown widget
disabledDates.name: disabledDates

weekendsDisabled.description:
Weekends are enabled by default
Disable sunday and saturday in the dropdown widget by setting the prop weekendsDisabled to true.
weekendsDisabled.name: weekendsDisabled

selectableDates.description:
Only dates that are contained in the list will be enabled in the dropdown widget.
Note: Some of these dates can be ruled out by the maxDate, minDate, weekendsDisabled rules.
selectableDates.name: selectableDates

refCallback.description: Callback function to get the ref of the button.
refCallback.name: refCallback

loadingText.description:
This prop is used to provide an ARIA label for the Spinner component to inform screen reader users about the current status of processes.
If not set, a fixed text ("Loading"/"Laden...") will be read by screen readers. Note that this text (also visible beneath the Spinner circle with reduced-motion browser settings)
cannot be modified to avoid potential UX issues caused by too long text.
loadingText.name: loadingText

isSelected.description:
Change appearance of the ChipItemAction to have a darker background color, for example
in a use case where the chip shows that there are filters active that can be removed by clicking on the chip.
Activates clear icon (if you don't need this, set the prop clearIcon to false).
isSelected.name: isSelected

onResetFilter.description: If ChipItemAction has an active selection, the selection has to be removable.
onResetFilter.name: onResetFilter

ariaDescription.description:
@deprecated 26.11.24: use ariaLabel instead, With this description any detailed information can be placed
to a circle diagram as aria-labelledby for users with a visual impairment.
ariaDescription.name: ariaDescription

ariaLabel.description:
a11y -relevant: With this description any detailed information can be placed to a circle diagram as
aria-labelledby for users with a visual impairment.
Note: It's recommended  to use
ariaLabel.name: ariaLabel

valueLabel.description: Text corresponding to the amount (mostly a percentage info like "66%" ).
valueLabel.name: valueLabel

valueAnimationCallback.description:
Callback on animation. Please use the callback with the parameter `progress` and multiply with 0 - 1. for value animation.
Second parameter ´amount:number´ will deprecate soon, because the second parameter is for internal purposes only and doesn't make sense on public .
Not relevant in a countdown scenario
valueAnimationCallback.name: valueAnimationCallback

label.description:
Small text below is useful in none countdown scenario and works as a
subline for valueLabel and an essential, additional information part
label.name: label

amount.description: Please pass a value between 0 and 1. Do not use in a countdown scenario
amount.name: amount

color.description:
Color of diagram, can be switched between 2 color aspects  (default: primary-1)
Useful for countdown scenario and other.
color.name: color

noAnimation.description: Set true for noAnimation. The value will appear directly after loading. (default = false)
noAnimation.name: noAnimation

animationOnStart.description:
On true the animation starts right after loading, even when the Circle is not in the viewport (default = false). Not relevant for countdown scenarios.
Prop is not relevant in a countdown scenario
animationOnStart.name: animationOnStart

headline.description: Headline is placed above the value, relevant for none countdown and countdown scenario
headline.name: headline

onRefreshClick.description: Callback which is triggered when the refresh button is clicked. Useful in a countdown scenario.
onRefreshClick.name: onRefreshClick

secondsUntilRefresh.description: If countdown is active, you can set how many seconds the countdown starts with. Useful in a countdown scenario.
secondsUntilRefresh.name: secondsUntilRefresh

countdown.description:
Activates a countdown, which can be reset by user (default is 5 seconds). Essential prop
in a countdown scenario
countdown.name: countdown

countDownFinishText.description:
This text become displayed in color red. Because the user should pay attention to the status when countdown run to zero.
The legacy message become provided as default when no other text is needed. But it can be overwritten. ( default: "Orderfrist abgelaufen" (de) / "Order period has exprired" (en)
Useful in a Countdown scenario.
countDownFinishText.name: countDownFinishText

callToActionButton.description: A button can be added to the CircleDiagram in a countdown scenario
callToActionButton.name: callToActionButton

chartInfoArea.description:
Chart information as presentation of amounts behind circle diagram chart expression. Prop is not intended for countdown scenarios, means value presentation only.
Not relevant for countdown scenarios.
chartInfoArea.name: chartInfoArea

sizeMode.description:
Size mode: `fixed` considers behavior so far, `dynamic` needs a limiting box around (parent element like div) to catch the size (default = fixed / with 480 px).
Useful for countdown scenarios and other.
sizeMode.name: sizeMode

iconVariant.description:
One of the presets defined in {Icon.Variants -> SOLID or OUTLINE}.
Have a look at the Icon component description for further information and visual examples.

loadingText.description:
This prop is used to provide an ARIA label for the Spinner component to inform screen reader users about the current status of processes.
If not set, a fixed text ("Loading"/"Laden...") will be read by screen readers. Note that this text (also visible beneath the Spinner circle with reduced-motion browser settings)
cannot be modified to avoid potential UX issues caused by too long text.
loadingText.name: loadingText

href.description:
External link address. An 'a'-tag will be used when you fill this property.
If there's no href, a button element will be used.
href.name: href

name.description: Name of the component, can be used in onClick handler
name.name: name

htmlAttrs.description: Additional HTML attributes can be added here (e.g. ARIA attributes, tabIndex, ...)
htmlAttrs.name: htmlAttrs

type.name: InputHTMLAttributes<HTMLInputElement> | Record<`data-${string}`, string | number | boolean> | ButtonHTMLAttributes<HTMLButtonElement> | AnchorHTMLAttributes<...>

onMouseDown.description: Function to be called before each onClick-Event. Not called when the button is disabled.
onMouseDown.name: onMouseDown

onClick.description:
Function to be called on the onClick event. This function will not be called if the link is disabled.
The `name` parameter will return the name you passed to the component.
onClick.name: onClick

onKeyDown.description: Function to be called whenever a key is pressed while the button is focused.
onKeyDown.name: onKeyDown

refCallback.description: Callback function to get the ref of the button.
refCallback.name: refCallback

actionAs.description:
Render an alternative action component, e.g. a React Router link
Example: <<Button, Teaser, etc.> actionAs={ReactRouterLink} actionProps={{to: "/mypage"}} ...
actionAs.name: actionAs

actionProps.description: These are the props for the alternative action component (e.g. a React Router link)
actionProps.name: actionProps

nonInteractive.description:
If true, this component will be decorative only and will not contain the `<a>` or `<button>` tag itself.
This is useful for non-interactive elements inside a card or other components.
nonInteractive.name: nonInteractive

a11yMeaningfulLabel.description:
If true, this component will animate the entire card or other components it is inside.
Make sure that the link text is meaningful (e.g. "Buy Product", "Cancel"). It must not contain general phrases
(e.g. "Read more", "Select").
a11yMeaningfulLabel.name: a11yMeaningfulLabel

as.description:
Name of the html tag that is rendered for the outer div. Defaults to "li" if used inside a group with more than
one item, or a group that is set to "ul" or "ol". To change the tag of the button, use the `actionAs` prop.
as.name: as

id.description: The unique id of the element
id.name: id

className.description: Custom CSS classes that should be applied
className.name: className

children.description: Inner components or text that should be displayed
children.name: children

label.description: Defines the text that is shown next to the Icon. If look is set to "no-text" it will be set as title-attribute to be read by screenreaders
label.name: label

iconName.description: The icon that shall be displayed within the iconlink - this will also be within the click-area
iconName.name: iconName

icon.description: The icon that shall be displayed within the iconlink - this will also be within the click-area
icon.name: icon

color.description: Set the background color primary (filled), secondary (not filled)
color.name: color

centeredLayout.description: Switch between left and centered layout.
centeredLayout.name: centeredLayout

as.description: Component to render the outer element
as.name: as

type.name: InputHTMLAttributes<HTMLInputElement> | Record<`data-${string}`, string | number | boolean> | ButtonHTMLAttributes<HTMLButtonElement> | AnchorHTMLAttributes<...>

label.description: Defines the text that is shown next to the Icon. If look is set to "no-text" it will be set as title-attribute to be read by screenreaders
label.name: label

iconName.description: The icon that shall be displayed within the iconlink - this will also be within the click-area
iconName.name: iconName

icon.description: The icon that shall be displayed within the iconlink - this will also be within the click-area
icon.name: icon

color.description: Set the background color primary (filled), secondary (not filled)
color.name: color

centeredLayout.description: Switch between left and centered layout.
centeredLayout.name: centeredLayout

as.description: Component to render the outer element
as.name: as

type.name: InputHTMLAttributes<HTMLInputElement> | Record<`data-${string}`, string | number | boolean> | ButtonHTMLAttributes<HTMLButtonElement> | AnchorHTMLAttributes<...>

spacing.description: Spacing between the click list content and the border of a row
spacing.name: spacing

label.description:
You can set a small text for the whole ClickList above your first ClickList.Item with this prop. Using this
prop also internally sets the aria-labelledby attribute. As a result, your label prop value will be linked to
the ClickList HTML element by aria-labelledby. (This will also overwrite any additional aria-labelledby if
you set one in the htmlAttrs prop of your ClickList.)
label.name: label

htmlAttrs.description: Additional HTML attributes can be added here (e.g. ARIA attributes, tabIndex, ...)
htmlAttrs.name: htmlAttrs

id.description: The unique id of the element
id.name: id

className.description: Custom CSS classes that should be applied
className.name: className

children.description: Inner components or text that should be displayed
children.name: children

statusIndicatorIcon.description: Sets an additional small icon as a status indicator.
statusIndicatorIcon.name: statusIndicatorIcon

showLinkLabel.description: Will specify if the link label is shown.
showLinkLabel.name: showLinkLabel

disabled.description: Is the item clickable by the user? This will also affect its appearance.
disabled.name: disabled

onClick.description: onClick mouse event
onClick.name: onClick

href.description: If you insert an href prop, an `a` tag will be rendered
href.name: href

htmlAttrs.description: Additional HTML attributes can be added here (e.g. ARIA attributes, tabIndex, ...)
htmlAttrs.name: htmlAttrs

type.name: Record<`data-${string}`, string | number | boolean> | ButtonHTMLAttributes<HTMLButtonElement> | AnchorHTMLAttributes<HTMLAnchorElement>

badgeText.description: Display a badge component in the TwoLineItem by adding a badge text.
badgeText.name: badgeText

badgeIcon.description: Display a badge component in the TwoLineItem by adding a badge icon.
badgeIcon.name: badgeIcon

badgeIconVariant.description:
Badge icons can be styled as outline, featuring hollow designs that emphasize contours, or solid, which are
filled in.
badgeIconVariant.name: badgeIconVariant

badgeScreenReaderLabel.description: Describe the Badge Content or add context to the badge message which will be read by screen readers
badgeScreenReaderLabel.name: badgeScreenReaderLabel

actionAs.description:
Render an alternative action component, e.g. a React Router link instead of an <a> or <button> tag
Example: <ClickListItem actionAs={ReactRouterLink} actionProps={{to: "/mypage"}} ...
actionAs.name: actionAs

actionProps.description: These are the props for the alternative action component (e.g. a React Router link)
actionProps.name: actionProps

badgeColor.description:
This prop sets the colour of the Badge. Please see our https://markenportal.commerzbank.com/styleguide/badge/index.html site for the
generated colour effects.
badgeColor.name: badgeColor

loading.description: Switch the item to loading mode. It will be disabled and a loading spinner will be shown.
loading.name: loading

loadingAriaLabel.description:
This prop is used to provide an ARIA label for the Spinner component to inform screen reader users about the current status of processes.
If not set, a fixed text ("Loading"/"Laden...") will be read by screen readers. Note that this text (also visible beneath the Spinner circle with reduced-motion browser settings)
cannot be modified to avoid potential UX issues caused by too long text.
loadingAriaLabel.name: loadingAriaLabel

headline.description:
First line text for your item. It has to be mandatory for screen readers. If a thumbnail icon is set, the
'headline' prop also describes it.
headline.name: headline

headlineAs.description:
HTML tag rendered for the surrounding tag (in some cases further out) of your first line text.
Set to "strong" by default for the directly-surrounding text tag of ClickList.Multiactions.Item and of a
ClickList with 'linkIcon' prop (overwritable by e.g. "p", "h5").
headlineAs.name: headlineAs

subline.description: If set, a subline will be rendered
subline.name: subline

sublineAs.description: An alternative HTML tag for the subline
sublineAs.name: sublineAs

statusColor.description:
This prop illustrates the current status with a coloured, small dot. The status value
"inactive" will show a dot in the colour of the surrounding Theme, the value "success" will show a green dot,
"warning" an orange dot, and "error" a red dot.
statusColor.name: statusColor

statusTag.description: This prop sets the status tag (label), which will be read by screen readers
statusTag.name: statusTag

thumbIconVariant.description:
Style variant for the thumbnail icon. Icons can be styled as outline, featuring hollow designs that emphasize
contours, or solid, which are filled in.
thumbIconVariant.name: thumbIconVariant

thumbIconTitle.description: You can set a title for your thumbnail icon with this prop.
thumbIconTitle.name: thumbIconTitle

thumbText.description: Text that should be placed inside the circle
thumbText.name: thumbText

id.description: The unique id of the element
id.name: id

className.description: Custom CSS classes that should be applied
className.name: className

children.description: Inner components or text that should be displayed
children.name: children

value.description: Sets the value of the ClickList.
value.name: value

name.description: Sets the name of the ClickList.
name.name: name

onChange.description: Called on each value change due to selection of a selectable item within the click list, providing the new value.
onChange.name: onChange

type.name: (value: string, name: string, event: SyntheticEvent<HTMLElement, Event>) => void

onBlur.description: onBlur will be thrown when the whole component lose the focus
onBlur.name: onBlur

invalid.description: Prop can be used as central prop on validation process in opposite to set invalid selectively on every item, if any value is mandatory
invalid.name: invalid

errorText.description: Prop can be used on validation process with providing error text below the list
errorText.name: errorText

value.description:
If you implemented a Checkbox, setting the value to true/false will check/uncheck the Checkbox.
Setting the value to undefined will generate the indeterminate look of the Checkbox (which is not meant to
be activated by a user clicking on or
pressing the Space key on a Checkbox). For information about indeterminate use cases,
please see https://css-tricks.com/indeterminate-checkboxes/ and
https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Element/input/checkbox. WARNING: The `ClickListRadios` parent
will override this prop.

"inactive" will show a dot in the colour of the surrounding Theme, the value "success" will show a green dot,
"warning" an orange dot, and "error" a red dot.
statusColor.name: statusColor

statusTag.description: This prop sets the status tag (label), which will be read by screen readers
statusTag.name: statusTag

thumbIconVariant.description:
Style variant for the thumbnail icon. Icons can be styled as outline, featuring hollow designs that emphasize
contours, or solid, which are filled in.
thumbIconVariant.name: thumbIconVariant

thumbIconTitle.description: You can set a title for your thumbnail icon with this prop.
thumbIconTitle.name: thumbIconTitle

thumbText.description: Text that should be placed inside the circle
thumbText.name: thumbText

spacing.name: spacing

label.description:
You can set a small text for the whole ClickList above your first ClickList.Item with this prop. Using this
prop also internally sets the aria-labelledby attribute. As a result, your label prop value will be linked to
the ClickList HTML element by aria-labelledby. (This will also overwrite any additional aria-labelledby if
you set one in the htmlAttrs prop of your ClickList.)
label.name: label

actions.name: actions

headlineAs.description:
HTML tag rendered for the surrounding tag (in some cases further out) of your first line text.
Set to "strong" by default for the directly-surrounding text tag of ClickList.Multiactions.Item and of a
ClickList with 'linkIcon' prop (overwritable by e.g. "p", "h5").
headlineAs.name: headlineAs

Setting the value to undefined will generate the indeterminate look of the Checkbox (which is not meant to
be activated by a user clicking on or
pressing the Space key on a Checkbox). For information about indeterminate use cases,
please see https://css-tricks.com/indeterminate-checkboxes/ and
https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Element/input/checkbox. WARNING: The `ClickListRadios` parent
will override this prop.

tags.deprecated: This component will not be further maintained and will not receive a11y updates. Use DataTable instead.

columnProperties.description:
An array of objects with the IComplexTableColumnProperties interface. The column properties correspond with the
tableBodyData "columns".
columnProperties.name: columnProperties

tableBodyData.description:
This array provides the table data. It is an array of table rows with column elements; each column element
corresponding to a columnProperties element.
tableBodyData.name: tableBodyData

onHeadingClick.description:
If the header of a sortable column is clicked, this callback handler returns the column id (starting with 0),
the column name as defined in the columnProperties, and the information if the column shall be
sorted ascending or descending.
onHeadingClick.name: onHeadingClick

type.name: (column: number, columnName: string, isSortAscending: boolean, e: MouseEvent<HTMLElement, MouseEvent>) => void

onBodyRowClick.description:
This callback handler returns the clicked row.
only called when there are no actions icons defined. Action icons should provide onclick handlers.
onBodyRowClick.name: onBodyRowClick

type.name: (rowIndex: number, rowId: string, e: MouseEvent<HTMLElement, MouseEvent>) => void

sortColumn.description:
Optional parameter: Shows by a black arrow that the table is sorted by the sortColumn with this column number (starting with 0).
In the non-Stateful variant, the user has to provide the sorting.
sortColumn.name: sortColumn

isSortedAscending.description:
Optional parameter: if a sortColumn parameter is provided, this flag is the information of the sort direction.
IsSortedAscendig == true shows an up arrow, IsSortedAscendig == false shows an down arrow.
isSortedAscending.name: isSortedAscending

description:
This is a stateful version of `<ComplexTable>`. PLEASE NOTE: Unlike the Stateful variants of formfield components
that are meant to be implemented for non-React-Environment (e.g. ShowCases), this variant of the `ComplexTable` is meant for React Logic also.
The `ComplexTable.Stateful` adds a sorting functionality, so you do not have necessarily to maintain the contents of the table and the sorting
algorithm in your wrapping component. For the sorting functionality, you can also define a sorting comparator for each column.

onHeadingClick.description:
If the header of a sortable column is clicked, this callback handler returns the column id (starting with 0),
the column name as defined in the columnProperties, and the information if the column shall be
sorted ascending or descending.
onHeadingClick.name: onHeadingClick

type.name: (column: number, columnName: string, isSortAscending: boolean, e: MouseEvent<HTMLElement, MouseEvent>) => void

IColumnResponsive: Header and column properties such as the title, text flow and responsive behaviour.
IColumnResponsive contains the attribute `widthFr` which allows for defining a column with
fraction unit (see Css Grid) values, if table prop `width`=`full`

description:
Comprehensive DatePicker automation test story
Tests all DatePicker scenarios including basic functionality, states, accessibility, and validation

Automation DatePicker test story that matches the expected test pattern.
Creates DatePicker variants with consistent IDs for automation testing.

helperText.name: helperText

Adds an error text below the component. If you use this prop, the errorText is added below the helperText (if
defined), and the component is implicitly set to invalid.

readOnly.name: readOnly

htmlAttrs.name: htmlAttrs

optional.name: optional

clearIcon.description: Sets the visibility of the clear icon. Default is true. A click will call the 'onChange' method.
clearIcon.name: clearIcon

minDate.description:
Date Object with the earliest date that can be selected. The minimum date is included in the selectable range.
Ensure, that minDate <= maxDate. Only year, month and day information are used, the time information is ignored.
minDate.name: minDate

maxDate.description:
Date Object with the latest date that can be selected. The maximum date is included in the selectable range.
Ensure, that minDate <= maxDate. Only year, month and day information are used, the time information is ignored.
maxDate.name: maxDate

disabledDates.description:
Set the disabledDates list which contains e.g. the business holiday days.
Days contained in the list will be displayed disabled in the dropdown widget.
With a DatePicker dateformat="month", give in a list of "firstOfMonth" dates to disable distinct dates.
This prop is ruled out by the "selectableDates" prop.
disabledDates.name: disabledDates

weekendsDisabled.description:
Weekends are enabled by default. Disable sunday and saturday in the dropdown widget by setting the prop
`weekendsDisabled` to true.
weekendsDisabled.name: weekendsDisabled

selectableDates.description:
Only dates that are contained in the list will be enabled in the dropdown widget.
Note: Some of these dates can be ruled out by the maxDate, minDate, weekendsDisabled rules.
With a DatePicker dateformat="month", give in a list of "firstOfMonth" dates to enable distinct dates.
This prop is ruled out by the "selectableDates" prop.
selectableDates.name: selectableDates

errorTextAriaLive.description: Use this prop to control if the aria-live attribute is set for the errorText
errorTextAriaLive.name: errorTextAriaLive

noYearPicker.description:
Opt-out for the year picker, that by default opens on click on the <month year> label on top of the
DatePicker flyout.
noYearPicker.name: noYearPicker

spacing.description:
With spacing="dense", the text input whitespace of the DatePicker is reduced by smaller paddings and bottom-margins.
Set spacing="default" only in special FormContainer with spacing="dense" prop (edge) cases where you wish to override the "dense" spacing
 in an individual DatePicker. In this case, the DatePicker will receive its default whitespace within the FormContainer.
spacing.name: spacing

Commerzbank or custom logos or assign your own.

Spacings that are applied on top and bottom of the Grid component. Inside nested grids, the spacings are
only applied at the bottom. Use "dense", "small", "medium" or "large". Other options will be removed in the next major
release.
If you do not set this prop, the default spacing style will be equivalent to the 'spacing="small"' prop version.
Note that this prop and the default spacing version will not have any effect if you use the Grid nested in `<FormContainer spacing="dense">`.

id.name: id

className.description: Custom CSS classes that should be applied
className.name: className

children.description: Inner components or text that should be displayed
children.name: children

size.description:
With this prop, you can choose your preferred CSS styling of the Headline. Together
with the `as` prop, you can create a correct HTML hierarchy independent of the visual appearance of your
Headline.
size.name: size

as.description:
With this prop, you have to set the HTML tag that will be rendered for the Headline.
The prop is mandatory to encourage the correct HTML tag hierarchy usage for your website.
Together with the `size` prop, you can create a correct hierarchy independent of the visual appearance
of your Headline. E.g. "h3", "h4", "strong", "span".
as.name: as

overlineAs.description:
HTML tag that is rendered for the overline. If you use the `overlineAs` prop to put the overline in a
semantically higher order (e.g. as='h2' and overlineAs='h1'), the overline will be rendered by its own
(`<h1 />`) above the headline (`<h2 />`) and not 'inside'.
overlineAs.name: overlineAs

sublineAs.description: HTML tag that is rendered for the subline.
sublineAs.name: sublineAs

balanced.description:
If true, applies text-wrap: balanced to the headline for improved readability.
By default, h1-h3 use balanced wrapping. h4-h6 in a centered layout are also balanced by default
balanced.name: balanced

lineLength.description: Sets the line length of '4' to '6' style (`size` prop) Headlines in ≥ 1024 px width viewports. Default is 768px. Wide is 960px.
lineLength.name: lineLength

centeredLayout.description: This prop centers the Headline horizontally.
centeredLayout.name: centeredLayout

manualHyphenation.description: Deactivate automatic hyphens if you want to set them manually.
manualHyphenation.name: manualHyphenation

badgeText.description: Badge text. Overwrites 'badgeIcon' prop if set as well.
badgeText.name: badgeText

badgeIcon.description: Badge Icon. If 'badgeText' prop is set as well, 'badgeIcon' will be ignored.
badgeIcon.name: badgeIcon

icon.description:
The Icon that should be displayed. See https://markenportal.commerzbank.com/styleguide/icon/
for an overview of available LSG Icons.
icon.name: icon

size.description: Scaled icon in four different sizes.
size.name: size

color.description: Pass one of the presets defined in literal type.
color.name: color

variant.description:
Icons can be styled as outline, featuring hollow designs that
emphasize contours, or solid, which are filled in.
variant.name: variant

title.description: Icon title. If title is not set, the corresponding or default title is used. Set `title=""` if you require an empty title.
title.name: title

inline.description: Whether to display it inline without a margin. Set to true if icon should be displayed inside continuous text.
inline.name: inline

svgAttrs.description: Additional attributes for the SVG element (e.g. aria attributes).
svgAttrs.name: svgAttrs

showBadge.description: Show a badge - Only available when icon size is "small".
showBadge.name: showBadge

badgeText.description:
Badge text
- If badgeText is undefined or an empty string, the Badge will be rendered as a dot.
- Up to 3 characters may fit inside. E.g. `99+`.
badgeText.name: badgeText

id.description: The unique id of the element
id.name: id

className.description: Custom CSS classes that should be applied
className.name: className

children.description: Inner components or text that should be displayed
children.name: children

description:
Interactive IconLink with all configurable properties.
Use the Controls panel to modify the component.
Various IconLink examples showing different configurations

label.description:
Defines the text displayed next to the icon. This prop is always required for accessibility reasons, even
when using `appearance="no-text"`: Screen readers will always announce this text, regardless of visual appearance.
The `label` text is rendered inside a <span> element and can be any valid React node (string, JSX, etc.).
label.name: label

appearance.description:
Use one of the defined IconLink appearances.
Accessible via union type.
appearance.name: appearance

hoverAnimation.description:
This prop sets the animation direction for hover and overrides the default of the Group. The prop should be set
for IconLinks with an arrow icon like `← Back` or `Next →`. In contrast to the Group default, the animation
direction set by this prop is independent of the orientation of IconLinks inside a group.
hoverAnimation.name: hoverAnimation

color.description:
Sets the color scheme for the IconLink text and icon. Accepts "primary" or "secondary" - if `iconColor` is
not set, the icon will inherit this color. The default is "primary". If the IconLink is disabled, the text and icon color will always be
set to "disabled", regardless of the `color` prop. The surrounding Theme influences the color that is rendered.
color.name: color

icon.description:
Icon of the Icon component.
See https://markenportal.commerzbank.com/styleguide/icon/ for an overview of available LSG Icons.
icon.name: icon

iconColor.description:
Sets the color of the icon independently from the text.
If not set, the icon color is determined by the `color` prop or the `disabled` state.
The surrounding Theme influences the color that is rendered.
iconColor.name: iconColor

iconVariant.description:
Icons can be styled as outline, featuring hollow designs that
emphasize contours, or solid, which are filled in.
Have a look on https://markenportal.commerzbank.com/styleguide/icon/ for further information and visual examples.
iconVariant.name: iconVariant

showBadge.description: A Badge is a small, rounded indicator on the top right side of the IconLink. The Badge may contain text.
showBadge.name: showBadge

badgeText.description:
If the badgeText is undefined, the Badge is shown as a small dot. If the Badge contains text (e.g. "7", "9+")
or an empty string, the Badge is larger. Up to 3 characters (e.g. "99+") may fit in a Badge.
badgeText.name: badgeText

inline.description:
Set this prop to true to render the IconLink as an inline element within text content (e.g., inside Paragraphs or Headlines).
When true, the outer container element changes from `<div>` to `<span>` to allow proper inline text flow.
This is useful when you want to place an IconLink within a sentence or text block.
inline.name: inline

nonInteractive.description:
If true, this component will be decorative only and will not contain the `<a>` or `<button>` tag itself.
This is useful for non-interactive elements inside a card or other components.
nonInteractive.name: nonInteractive

a11yMeaningfulLabel.description:
If true, this component will animate the entire card or other components it is inside.
Make sure that the link text is meaningful (e.g. "Buy Product", "Cancel"). It must not contain general phrases
(e.g. "Read more", "Select").
a11yMeaningfulLabel.name: a11yMeaningfulLabel

as.description:
Name of the HTML tag that is rendered for the outer div. Defaults to "li" if used inside a group with more than
one item, or a group that is set to "ul" or "ol". To change the tag of the button, use the `actionAs` prop.
as.name: as

disabled.description: Is the Button clickable by the user? This also reflects in its appearance.
disabled.name: disabled

loading.description: Switches the Button to loading mode. It gets disabled and a loading Spinner will be shown.
loading.name: loading

loadingText.description:
This prop is used to provide an ARIA label for the Spinner component to inform screen reader users about the current status of processes.
If not set, a fixed text ("Loading"/"Laden...") will be read by screen readers. Note that this text (also visible beneath the Spinner circle with reduced-motion browser settings)
cannot be modified to avoid potential UX issues caused by too long text.
loadingText.name: loadingText

href.description:
External link address. An 'a'-tag will be used when you fill this property.
If there's no href, a button element will be used.
href.name: href

name.description: Name of the component, can be used in onClick handler
name.name: name

htmlAttrs.description: Additional HTML attributes can be added here (e.g. ARIA attributes, tabIndex, ...)
htmlAttrs.name: htmlAttrs

type.name: InputHTMLAttributes<HTMLInputElement> | Record<`data-${string}`, string | number | boolean> | ButtonHTMLAttributes<HTMLButtonElement> | AnchorHTMLAttributes<...>

onMouseDown.description: Function to be called before each onClick-Event. Not called when the button is disabled.
onMouseDown.name: onMouseDown

onClick.description:
Function to be called on the onClick event. This function will not be called if the link is disabled.
The `name` parameter will return the name you passed to the component.
onClick.name: onClick

onKeyDown.description: Function to be called whenever a key is pressed while the button is focused.
onKeyDown.name: onKeyDown

refCallback.description: Callback function to get the ref of the button.
refCallback.name: refCallback

actionAs.description:
Render an alternative action component, e.g. a React Router link
Example: <<Button, Teaser, etc.> actionAs={ReactRouterLink} actionProps={{to: "/mypage"}} ...
actionAs.name: actionAs

actionProps.description: These are the props for the alternative action component (e.g. a React Router link)
actionProps.name: actionProps

id.description: The unique id of the element
id.name: id

className.description: Custom CSS classes that should be applied
className.name: className

children.description: Inner components or text that should be displayed
children.name: children

direction.description: The layout direction of the group, can only be values from enum DIRECTIONS
direction.name: direction

htmlAttrs.description: Additional HTML attributes can be added here (e.g. ARIA attributes, tabIndex, ...)
htmlAttrs.name: htmlAttrs

as.description:
Name of the html tag that is rendered for the group container. If set to "ul" or "ol", the `as`-prop of
children will be set to "li". Default is "ul", if the group contains more than one IconLink.
as.name: as

tags.deprecated:
: 11.2023 - Will be removed in the next major release.
Have a look at the "Info List Pattern" which uses native components like shown in the Markenportal UX Patterns
section: https://markenportal.commerzbank.com/styleguide/info-list-pattern/index.html

singleColumn.description: Set property to display items always in one column.
singleColumn.name: singleColumn

id.description: The unique id of the element
id.name: id

className.description: Custom CSS classes that should be applied
className.name: className

children.description: Inner components or text that should be displayed
children.name: children

direction.description: Layout of the inputs, vertical or horizontal
direction.name: direction

label.description:
You can set a small title text for the whole group above your first item with
this prop. Using this prop also internally sets the aria-labelledby attribute. As a result, your label prop
value will be linked to the fieldset HTML element of the group by aria-labelledby. (However, setting an
additional aria-labelledby in the htmlAttrs prop of your group will overwrite the aria-labelledby set by
your label prop.)
label.name: label

helperText.description: Sets a helper text below the component.
helperText.name: helperText

errorText.description: Adds an error text below the component. If you use this prop, the errorText is added below the helperText (if defined), and the group is implicitly set to invalid.
errorText.name: errorText

invalid.description: Indicates if this group is valid
invalid.name: invalid

htmlAttrs.name: htmlAttrs

You can set the internal name attribute for the 'input type="radio"' HTML child elements with this prop. Several
radios with the same name will be grouped together. For more usage information, see

this prop. Using this prop also internally sets the aria-labelledby attribute. As a result, your label prop
value will be linked to the fieldset HTML element of the group by aria-labelledby. (However, setting an
additional aria-labelledby in the htmlAttrs prop of your group will overwrite the aria-labelledby set by
your label prop.)
label.name: label

errorText.name: errorText

invalid.description: Indicates if this group is valid
invalid.name: invalid

htmlAttrs.name: htmlAttrs

This prop is used to provide an ARIA label for the Spinner component to inform screen reader users about the current status of processes.
If not set, a fixed text ("Loading"/"Laden...") will be read by screen readers. Note that this text (also visible beneath the Spinner circle with reduced-motion browser settings)
cannot be modified to avoid potential UX issues caused by too long text.

  active?: boolean, (this sets this item active / sets the indicator on this item)
  indicator?: boolean, (this enables an indicator for the children of this item)
  id?: string,
  label: string,
  icon?: string,
  href?: string,
  onClick?: (ev: React.MouseEvent<any>) => void,
  children?: INavigationItem[] (an array of more objects looking like this one)
}

ariaLabel.description: Aria label for the slider knob(s) - either a string (single slider) or an array with two string values (range slider). Defaults to label and in case of range slider: label plus a default text indicating min/max value.

This is the value you have to provide; set it with the value of the onChange event.
In combination with rangeSlider flag, the value accept tuple with lower and upper value [lower, upper]

ariaValueTextFormatter.description:
Screen readers speak the current value when changed e.g. 1000. To improve a11y, you can change this text by this formatter, e.g. to "1000 €" (instead of the value 1000, which would be read by a screen reader)
In a multi range look, map the upper and lower value to an array with the screen reader friendly texts.

precision.description: If you need a value accurate to one decimal places, you should set the value of step to e.g. 0.01. To receive a value without decimal places, set the value to 1 .
precision.name: precision

onInput.name: onInput

Sets a role attribute on the tag element to ensure the correct a11y. Set to `role="status"` if the status is
changed interactively, while the page is opened by the user. Then, a change of the `tag` or `statusColor`
prop will produce an announcement to screen reader users. Make sure, that it is not set on with `<StatusIndicator>`s
inside a dynamically changing container (e.g. inside a sortable table).
For more information
@see  : https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/Accessibility/ARIA/Roles/status_role

developer guidelines.
Check `<Tabs>` for implementation examples.

disabled.description: Is the Button clickable by the user? This also reflects in its appearance.
disabled.name: disabled

loading.description: Switches the Button to loading mode. It gets disabled and a loading Spinner will be shown.
loading.name: loading

loadingText.description:
This prop is used to provide an ARIA label for the Spinner component to inform screen reader users about the current status of processes.
If not set, a fixed text ("Loading"/"Laden...") will be read by screen readers. Note that this text (also visible beneath the Spinner circle with reduced-motion browser settings)
cannot be modified to avoid potential UX issues caused by too long text.
loadingText.name: loadingText

href.description:
External link address. An 'a'-tag will be used when you fill this property.
If there's no href, a button element will be used.
href.name: href

htmlAttrs.name: htmlAttrs

onMouseDown.name: onMouseDown

onClick.description:
Function to be called on the onClick event. This function will not be called if the link is disabled.
The `name` parameter will return the name you passed to the component.
onClick.name: onClick

onKeyDown.description: Function to be called whenever a key is pressed while the button is focused.
onKeyDown.name: onKeyDown

refCallback.description: Callback function to get the ref of the button.
refCallback.name: refCallback

actionAs.description:
Render an alternative action component, e.g. a React Router link
Example: <<Button, Teaser, etc.> actionAs={ReactRouterLink} actionProps={{to: "/mypage"}} ...
actionAs.name: actionAs

actionProps.description: These are the props for the alternative action component (e.g. a React Router link)
actionProps.name: actionProps

nonInteractive.description:
If true, this component will be decorative only and will not contain the `<a>` or `<button>` tag itself.
This is useful for non-interactive elements inside a card or other components.
nonInteractive.name: nonInteractive

a11yMeaningfulLabel.description:
If true, this component will animate the entire card or other components it is inside.
Make sure that the link text is meaningful (e.g. "Buy Product", "Cancel"). It must not contain general phrases
(e.g. "Read more", "Select").
a11yMeaningfulLabel.name: a11yMeaningfulLabel

as.description:
Name of the html tag that is rendered for the outer div. Defaults to "li" if used inside a group with more than
one item, or a group that is set to "ul" or "ol". To change the tag of the button, use the `actionAs` prop.
Name of the HTML tag that is rendered for the outer div. Defaults to "li" if used inside a TeaserGroup.

id.description: The unique id of the element
id.name: id

className.description: Custom CSS classes that should be applied
className.name: className

children.description: Inner components or text that should be displayed
children.name: children

disabled.description: Disables all actions
disabled.name: disabled

onChange.description:
@deprecated Called on change, triggered by letting go of a slider knob or change in a text field. Provides a simple number in single slider mode, an array of two numbers  [lower, upper] in range slider mode.
Note: this handler is not called when you delete a value in a text field. To handle empty text inputs in a better way, use onInputChange instead.
onChange.name: onChange

onInputChange.description:
Called on change, triggered by letting go of a slider knob or change in a text field. Provides a simple number (or empty string) in single slider mode, an array of two values  [lower, upper] in range slider mode
The behavior is the same as in the onChange handler, but also reflects empty text fields. If a text field is empty, an empty string is returned as a value, so you can set an appropriate error text.
This behaviour is analogous to the onChange handler in the Text Field of type "number".
onInputChange.name: onInputChange

onSliderInput.description: Called on drag and value change events. Similar to onInputChange, but is also called while dragging the slider. The value contains one number value on single slider, two values in an array on range slider with [lower, upper]
onSliderInput.name: onSliderInput

onInputClose.description:
Event is raised when the user tries to close the text field area by click on the "return to slider" button or by enter key.
In this case, the text field is not necessarily closed because you set invalidInput to true.
Also, for this event you can do a validation on your own and if necessary, set the invalid input prop to false.
onInputClose.name: onInputClose

onInputOpen.description: Event is raised when the user opens the text input area in order to enter values into the text fields.
onInputOpen.name: onInputOpen

value.description: Current simple value or lower | upper value
value.name: value

name.description: This is the name of the component.
name.name: name

label.description: Label on top of the lower input field (required).
label.name: label

labelTo.description: Label for connection word between text fields or display field
labelTo.name: labelTo

helperMinValue.description: Text on the left bottom of the slider.
helperMinValue.name: helperMinValue

helperMaxValue.description: Text on the right bottom of the slider.
helperMaxValue.name: helperMaxValue

helperTextValue.description: Helper text below the text field, when the slider is hidden. In case of a range slider, insert an array of two string values. In case of an invalid text field, you have to set an errorText prop. In this case, you can unset the helperText of the field or set both helperText and errorText.
helperTextValue.name: helperTextValue

step.description: Steps are visually displayed on the slider. The step prop overrides the sliderStep prop. Keep in mind to use large steps in terms of good design. If that's not possible, use the sliderStep prop
step.name: step

precision.description: This is the value accuracy when you use the slider. Example: If you need a value accurate to 0 decimals (e.g. rounded to 1 Euro) set the step to 1 (default), if accurate to e.g. cent, set a sliderStep of 0.01 and if you need a value rounded to 100, set the sliderStep to 100.
precision.name: precision

formatter.description: Callback function for pretty-printing of displaying values
formatter.name: formatter

ariaValueTextFormatter.description: Formatter for current value, screen reader friendly. In a multi range look, map toan array with the current lower and upper aria value text e.g. ["1000 €", "3000 €"]
ariaValueTextFormatter.name: ariaValueTextFormatter

ariaSliderLabel.description: Aria label for the slider knob(s) - either a string (single slider) or an array with two string values (range slider). Defaults to label and in case of range slider: label plus a default text indicating min/max value.
ariaSliderLabel.name: ariaSliderLabel

ariaTextFieldLabel.description: Aria label for the text field(s) - either a string (single value) or an array with two string values (range values). Defaults to label and in case of range values: label plus a default text indicating min/max value.
ariaTextFieldLabel.name: ariaTextFieldLabel

decimalDigits.description: Number of decimal digits that are appended when switching to the text input, e.g. 2 for € with cents.
decimalDigits.name: decimalDigits

look.description: Decision which slider variation should be used
look.name: look

invalidInput.description: Input validation element: Responsible prop which reflects that an input is not valid. This prop is also implicitly set if you insert an errorText prop.
invalidInput.name: invalidInput

invalidInputField.description: Input validation element: Prop specifies the affected field that is not valid. This prop is also implicitly set if you insert a corresponding errorText prop.
invalidInputField.name: invalidInputField

errorText.description: Input validation element: Provides the error text beneath invalid input fields. In case of the range slider variant, insert an array of two string values.
errorText.name: errorText

preventAutoCorrection.description: On blur, incorrect values in input field(s) are restored to the last values by triggering an onInputChange event. You can prevent this behaviour by this prop. If this prop is set, the input field area will not close if the values in the text fields are incorrect.
preventAutoCorrection.name: preventAutoCorrection

disabled.name: disabled

label.description: Label on top of the slider. The label is mandatory for accessibility reasons.
label.name: label

inputTextfieldLabel.description: Label for the text field(s) - either a string (single value) or an array with two string values (range values). Is set to label by default and in case of range values: label plus a default text indicating min/max value.
inputTextfieldLabel.name: inputTextfieldLabel

helperMinValue.description: Text on the left bottom of the slider.
helperMinValue.name: helperMinValue

helperMaxValue.description: Text on the right bottom of the slider.
helperMaxValue.name: helperMaxValue

helperTextValue.description: Helper text for the text field, when the slider is hidden. Either a string (for a single slider) or an array with two string values (range slider). In case of an invalid text field, you have to set an errorText prop. In this case, you can unset the helperText of the field or set both helperText and errorText.
helperTextValue.name: helperTextValue

step.description: Steps are visually displayed on the slider. The step prop overrides the sliderStep prop. Keep in mind to use large steps in terms of good design. If that's not possible, use the sliderStep prop
step.name: step

precision.description: This is the value accuracy when you use the slider. Example: If you need a value accurate to 0 decimals (e.g. rounded to 1 Euro) set the step to 1 (default), if accurate to e.g. cent, set a sliderStep of 0.01 and if you need a value rounded to 100, set the sliderStep to 100.
precision.name: precision

sliderStep.description: @deprecated This is the value accuracy when you use the slider. Use precision instead!!
sliderStep.name: sliderStep

decimalDigits.description: Number of decimal digits that are appended when switching to displaying text input, e.g. 2 for € with cents.
decimalDigits.name: decimalDigits

onChange.description:
@deprecated Called on change, triggered by letting go of a slider knob or change in a text field. Provides a simple number in single slider mode, an array of two numbers  [lower, upper] in range slider mode.
Note: this handler is not called when you delete a value in a text field. To handle empty text inputs in a better way, use onInputChange instead.
onChange.name: onChange

onInputChange.description:
Called on change, triggered by letting go of a slider knob or change in a text field. Provides a simple number (or empty string) in single slider mode, an array of two values  [lower, upper] in range slider mode
The behavior is the same as in the (deprecated) onChange handler, but also reflects empty text fields. If a text field is empty, an empty string is returned as a value so you can set an appropriate error text.
This behaviour is analogous to the onChange handler in the Text Field of type "number".
Note: Do not use the onChange handler at the same time to prevent side effects.
onInputChange.name: onInputChange

onSliderInput.description: Called on drag and value change events of the slider and is not called on change of the text fields. The value contains one number value on single slider, two values in an array on range slider with [lower, upper]
onSliderInput.name: onSliderInput

onInputClose.description:
Event is raised when the user tries to close the text field area by click on the "return to slider" button or by enter key.
In this case, the text field area is not necessarily closed when you set invalidInput to true.
Also, for this event you can do a validation on your own and if necessary, set the invalid input prop to false.
onInputClose.name: onInputClose

onInputOpen.description: Event is raised when the user opens the text input area in order to enter values into the text fields.
onInputOpen.name: onInputOpen

value.description: In the value accept single value(number | "") or an array with lower and upper value [number | "", number | ""]
value.name: value

formatter.description: Callback function for pretty-printing a value
formatter.name: formatter

ariaValueTextFormatter.description:
Screen readers speak the current value when changed e.g. 1000. To improve a11y, you can change this text by this formatter, e.g. to "1000 €" (instead of the value 1000, which would be read by a screen reader)
In a multi range look, map the upper and lower value to an array with the screen reader friendly texts.
ariaValueTextFormatter.name: ariaValueTextFormatter

ariaSliderLabel.description: Aria label for the slider knob(s) - either a string (single slider) or an array with two string values (range slider). Is set to label by default and in case of range slider: label plus a default text indicating min/max value.
ariaSliderLabel.name: ariaSliderLabel

ariaTextFieldLabel.description: Aria label for the text field(s) - either a string (single value) or an array with two string values (range values). Is set to label by default and in case of range values: label plus a default text indicating min/max value.
ariaTextFieldLabel.name: ariaTextFieldLabel

labelTo.description: Label for a connection word between lower and upper text fields or display field in range slider mode
labelTo.name: labelTo

invalidInput.description: Input validation element: Responsible prop which reflects that a text field input is not valid. If you set this prop to true, the text input area won't close. This prop is also implicitly set if you insert an errorText prop.
invalidInput.name: invalidInput

invalidInputField.description: Input validation element: Prop specifies the affected field that is not valid in range mode. In single slider mode, the affected field will be marked automatically. This prop is also implicitly set if you insert a corresponding errorText prop.
invalidInputField.name: invalidInputField

errorText.description: Input validation element: Provides the error text beneath the input field(s). In case of the range slider variant, insert an array of two string values.
errorText.name: errorText

preventAutoCorrection.description: On blur, incorrect values in input field(s) are restored to the last values by triggering an onInputChange event. You can prevent this behaviour by this prop. If this prop is set, the input field area will not close if the values in the text fields are incorrect.
preventAutoCorrection.name: preventAutoCorrection

iconVariant.description:
Icons can be styled as outline, featuring hollow designs that emphasize contours, or solid, which are filled
in.
iconVariant.name: iconVariant

iconTitle.description:
Text used as the title attribute for the icon's SVG or image tag.
This text is displayed as a tooltip when the user hovers over the icon or the image.
If you do not set this prop (decorative icon), the icon will be aria-hidden and not accessible for screen readers.
iconTitle.name: iconTitle

text.description: Text that should be placed inside the circle
text.name: text

label.description:
Text of the first line. It has to be mandatory for screen readers because the label describes the thumbnail
next to it.
label.name: label

labelAs.description:
HTML tag that is rendered for the bold text of the first line (label). E.g. "h4", "h5", "strong". For
further information on which one to choose, see e.g.
https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Element/strong.
labelAs.name: labelAs

sublineAs.description: HTML tag that is rendered for the subline text. E.g. "pre" for two-rowed use cases.
sublineAs.name: sublineAs

centeredLayout.description: This prop centers the component horizontally if set to true.
centeredLayout.name: centeredLayout

badgeText.description: Badge text
badgeText.name: badgeText

badgeIcon.description: Badge icon
badgeIcon.name: badgeIcon

badgeScreenReaderLabel.description: Describe the Badge content or add context to the Badge message which will be read by screen readers.
badgeScreenReaderLabel.name: badgeScreenReaderLabel

manualHyphenation.description: Deactivate automatic hyphens if you want to set them manually.
manualHyphenation.name: manualHyphenation

appearance.description: Set this prop if only the thumbnail should be visible without any text (label and subline).
appearance.name: appearance

Returns event with FileList which can be iterated with for...of
https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/HTMLInputElement/files
onChange.name: onChange

multipleFiles.description: Set to true to allow multiple file uploads at once
multipleFiles.name: multipleFiles

handleFileUpload.description:
Unified interface for the file, because onChange events vary if the file is dragged into our select by the
user.
handleFileUpload.name: handleFileUpload

inputRef.description: You can use the inputRef to reset the Upload field if needed
inputRef.name: inputRef

acceptedFileTypes.description:
Optional array of accepted file types for the file input. This is a suggestion and does not enforce strict
limitations on the file types that can be uploaded.
acceptedFileTypes.name: acceptedFileTypes

invalid.description: Sets aria-invalid={true} on the input
invalid.name: invalid

ariaLabel.description: Aria-label which is set to the input element. Important: Set this to be accessible
ariaLabel.name: ariaLabel
